prompt: The parallel stream produces inconsistent results due to non-thread-safe collection. What is the bug?
choices: ArrayList is not thread-safe; parallel forEach() writing to it from multiple threads causes data corruption, so result.size() may be less than 1000 or throw an exception
forEach() in parallel streams always produces results in order
Parallel streams require the collection to implement Serializable
explanation: ArrayList.add() is not thread-safe. Concurrent writes from multiple threads cause race conditions: lost updates, wrong size, or ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException internally. Fix: use Collectors.toList() which handles thread safety: result = IntStream.range(0,1000).parallel().boxed().collect(Collectors.toList());
